---
title: "Symbiont counts check"
author: "Jill Ashey"
date: "2026-04-06"
output: html_document
---

Checking manual sym count values vs guava counts

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Read in manual counts
```{r}
sym_manual <- read.csv("../data/Physiology/sym_counts/manual_sym_counts.csv", header = T)
```

Calculate cells per square cm, normalize count by homogenate vol and surface area 
```{r}

blah_fixed <- sym_manual %>%
  # Make dilution numeric and treat NA as 1 (undiluted)
  mutate(Dilution = ifelse(is.na(Dilution), 1, Dilution)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Colony_ID, Timepoint, Squares.Counted, Dilution, matches("Count[0-9]")) %>%
  # Pivot counts to calculate the mean across replicates
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("Count"), names_to = "rep", values_to = "val") %>%
  group_by(Colony_ID, Timepoint, Squares.Counted, Dilution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean_val = mean(val, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  # Apply the hemocytometer formula
  mutate(
    # (Cells / Squares) * 10,000 * Dilution
    cells.mL = (mean_val / Squares.Counted) * 10000 * Dilution,
    # Total cells in the 10mL resuspension
    cells = cells.mL * 10, 
    cells.cm2 = cells / 15,
    cells.cm2.M = cells.cm2 / 1e6
  )
```
